behaviors.description: Scoring model assigns the correct risk score when transaction amount exceeds configured threshold for that merchant category.
behaviors.justification: This can fail at the boundary between category-to-rule config lookup and scoring logic. A pure unit test of arithmetic can miss real wiring bugs (wrong category key, stale config mapping, default-rule fallback).
behaviors.test_description: Run scoring against a local rules/config store containing at least two merchant categories with different thresholds. Submit a transaction that exceeds only its category threshold, then assert the returned numeric score reflects the configured rule weight for that category (and not another category/default).

behaviors.description: Risk model identifies a fraudulent pattern (rapid small purchases across geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness here is model judgment quality, not deterministic rule wiring. Mocking the model would hide misclassification risk and cannot validate real model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real risk-model inference on a labeled fraud-like sequence (small amounts, short intervals, distant geos) constructed to stay below scoring thresholds. Assert fraud probability crosses the fraud decision threshold. Run multiple times with acceptance bands/repeat criteria to account for model variance.

behaviors.description: Velocity check counts transactions within configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly come from time-window query boundaries, timestamp normalization, and per-user filtering across storage/query modules. In-process mocks can miss these.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local transaction store with events around the exact window boundaries for multiple users. Execute velocity scoring for one user and assert the count includes/excludes boundary timestamps per spec and ignores other users.

behaviors.description: Transaction flagged by risk model but not scoring model is still held for review (OR gate semantics).
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is model-output integration into decisioning/hold logic. Unit tests can miss contract mismatches (field names/status encoding) between components.
behaviors.test_description: Run decision service with real wiring between scoring output, risk output, and review-hold decision module. Provide input where scoring result is below hold threshold and risk result is flagged. Assert final disposition is HOLD_FOR_REVIEW.

behaviors.description: Combined output includes numeric risk score and risk-model fraud probability so reviewer can see which model flagged and why.
behaviors.justification: This is an output-contract composition issue across modules (scoring, risk, formatter/DTO). Unit tests can miss dropped/renamed fields during integration/serialization.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end composition path (without mocking output formatter) and assert response payload contains: numeric `risk_score`, numeric `fraud_probability`, per-model flag indicators, and explanatory reason fields mapped to the model that triggered review.
